topic: My GPS keeps telling me to take an exit that does not exist
title: My GPS Keeps Telling Me to Take an Exit That Does Not Exist | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: I drive the same stretch of I-seventy every weekday for work.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot through a windshield of a straight midwestern interstate highway at early morning, flat farmland on both sides, pale sunrise on the horizon, light traffic, overhead highway signs in the distance, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Same route. Four years. No surprises.
scenes.image_prompt: interior shot of a car dashboard at dawn, GPS unit mounted on the windshield showing a familiar route line, coffee in the cupholder, flat empty highway ahead, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Then one morning: exit one forty seven.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a car GPS screen showing a turn instruction, the display indicating an upcoming exit in four hundred feet, the road on the map showing an off-ramp that branches into nothing, ambient morning light on the screen, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I knew that mile. No exit there.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot through a windshield of a stretch of interstate highway, concrete barriers on both sides, no off-ramp visible, overhead gantry signs showing only the next major exit at a higher number, morning light flat, vertical 9:16 framing

close shot of a GPS unit mounted on a windshield, the screen showing a recalculating message, the map redrawing, sunlight reflecting off the corner of the screen, highway visible through the glass behind it, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: It happened every day that week.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a car GPS screen with the same exit instruction displayed, the screen slightly brighter in the dim car interior, a hand visible on the steering wheel in the lower frame, the highway stretching ahead, vertical 9:16 framing

close shot of a hand holding a smartphone to an ear in a parked car interior, afternoon light through the windshield, the GPS unit still mounted and lit on the dashboard, the screen showing the same stretch of highway, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: No exit one forty seven in their database. Never was.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing a highway mapping application with a stretch of interstate displayed, the mile markers visible, a gap in the exit list between one forty six and one forty eight, no intermediate entry, dim desk light around the screen, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Last Thursday the GPS said turn now and I turned.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot through a windshield at dusk of a car turning onto a narrow gravel road from an interstate on-ramp that should not exist, the gravel road disappearing into dark trees ahead, the interstate visible receding behind, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Gravel road. Half a mile in. A concrete foundation in the field. Fresh tire tracks going in. No tracks coming out.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot from a car stopped on a gravel road at dusk, headlights illuminating a clearing ahead where a concrete foundation slab sits in overgrown grass, tire tracks in the soft earth leading up to the slab, no tracks visible returning, dark tree line behind, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I reversed out without leaving the car.
scenes.image_prompt: interior rear-view mirror shot inside a car reversing at dusk, the gravel road visible receding ahead through the back window, the headlights illuminating the road behind, the foundation slab just barely visible at the edge of the headlight range, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The GPS has not shown exit one forty seven since. The tire tracks were mine.
scenes.image_prompt: wide exterior shot of an empty gravel pull-off beside an interstate highway at dawn, tire tracks in soft earth leading a short distance and stopping, flat farmland beyond a wire fence, the highway visible in the background, pale morning light, vertical 9:16 framing
